metadata.description: Dream interpretation traditions across world cultures. Each entry includes core beliefs, symbol systems, and practices. Designed to enable culturally-aware AI dream interpretation.

cultures.name: Chinese - Zhou Gong's Dream Dictionary
cultures.era: Western Zhou Dynasty (~1046-771 BCE), compiled and expanded through Ming/Qing dynasties
core_beliefs: Dreams are messages from heaven and ancestors, serving as guidance and warnings for the dreamer
The relationship between dream and reality is often inverted: dreaming of death may signify longevity, dreaming of crying may predict joy
Dreams are influenced by seasonal qi and the five elements (五行), with different seasons producing different dream types
The timing of the dream within the night cycle affects its significance and likelihood of fulfillment
Dreams reflect the balance or imbalance of yin and yang energies in the dreamer's body and life
practices: Dream interpretation was integrated into imperial governance; court dream interpreters advised emperors
Recording dreams at dawn before daily activities to preserve accuracy
Consulting dream dictionaries (梦书) organized by category for standardized interpretation
Dream incubation in temples, particularly at sacred sites dedicated to Zhou Gong
Using dreams to guide medical diagnosis in conjunction with TCM pulse reading

symbols.meaning: Imperial power, great fortune, promotion. Riding a dragon means imminent success
Wealth and fertility. A snake entering the home predicts financial gain
Abundance and surplus (余). Catching fish means incoming wealth
Longevity, stability, and protection. Also a sign of slow but certain progress
Auspiciousness, harmony, and feminine power. Predicts marriage or partnership
Authority, power, and respect. Being chased by a tiger means a powerful person will assist you
Good luck arriving; the Chinese word for spider (蜘蛛/喜蛛) contains the character for happiness
Blessing and good fortune (蝠 sounds like 福/fortune)
Treachery or hidden enemies. A cat catching a mouse means resolution of conflict
Loyalty and friendship. A barking dog warns of gossip or slander
Speed of success. A galloping horse means rapid advancement
Diligence rewarded. An ox plowing a field predicts hard work leading to harvest
Dawn and new beginnings. A crowing rooster announces good news
Wealth and material comfort. A fat pig signifies prosperity
Immortality and transcendence. A crane flying means spiritual elevation
Minor annoyances accumulating. Many ants means small troubles gathering
Promotion and wealth (棺 sounds like 官/official, 材 sounds like 财/wealth)
Wealth and emotion. Clear water means clarity; muddy water means confusion and trouble
Prosperity and passion when controlled; disaster when out of control
Obstacles to overcome, but also protection and stability. Climbing successfully means achievement
Nourishment and renewal. Light rain is blessings; heavy storm is emotional turmoil
Purity and new beginnings. Melting snow means troubles dissolving
Romance and beauty. Blooming flowers mean love arriving; wilting flowers mean relationship decline
Family lineage and personal growth. A flourishing tree means family prosperity
Feminine energy, reunion, and homesickness. A full moon predicts family gathering
Yang energy, success, and vitality. A rising sun means new opportunities
Life's journey. Crossing a river means overcoming a major obstacle
Major life upheaval and transformation. Not necessarily negative
Family member illness or death, particularly elders. One of the most feared dream omens
New project or creative endeavor beginning. Abundance coming
Letting go of old thinking patterns. Fresh start
Financial loss or emotional drain. The location of bleeding specifies the area of loss
Paradoxically positive: old situations ending, new phase beginning. Ancestors offering guidance
Partnership or commitment. May indicate business partnership rather than romantic
Self-evaluation and judgment. Failing an exam paradoxically may predict success
Liberation from constraints, ambition, and spiritual aspiration
Loss of control or status. The height indicates the severity
Avoiding a responsibility or truth that must be faced
Nourishment needs being met. What you eat matters: meat means wealth, vegetables mean health
Major life transition. The condition of the new house indicates the quality of the change
Solution to a problem. Finding a key means discovering an answer
Self-reflection and truth. A broken mirror means relationship rupture
Energy exchange. Finding money means incoming opportunities; losing money means releasing attachments
Transition between life phases. A broken bridge means an obstacle in transition
Opportunity. An open door means opportunities available; a locked door means timing is not right
Joy, celebration, good fortune. A deeply auspicious color in all dream contexts
Mourning, death, endings. Opposite of Western association with purity
Mystery, the unknown, yin energy. Not inherently negative
Wealth, imperial authority, divine favor
Guidance from lineage. Ancestors in dreams are delivering important messages that must be heeded
New beginnings, innocence, or a new project in its earliest stage
Wisdom and accumulated experience. An unknown elder may represent a guide figure
Social identity and reputation. New clothes mean improved status
Life journey and emotional navigation. Calm sailing means smooth progress
Social climbing, spiritual ascent. Each rung represents a step toward a goal
Decisiveness and the need to cut something from one's life
Enlightenment, hope, and guidance. A flickering lamp warns of uncertainty
Knowledge and examination. An open book means learning opportunity
Deep knowledge, the unconscious, and hidden resources
Integrity, resilience, and scholarly achievement
Purity emerging from adversity. Spiritual awakening
Transient worries. Clouds parting means clarity returning
Sudden revelation or shock. Also authority making itself known
Harmony of opposites. Good fortune after difficulty
Life force and spiritual light. A candle going out warns of health concerns
Aspirations and freedom. A kite flying high means ambitions will be realized
Partnership and cooperation. Broken chopsticks mean separation
Protection and shelter. Also separation (伞 contains 散/scatter)
Virtue, purity, and high moral character. Receiving jade means recognition of worth
Authority and official recognition. Gaining power or approval
Celebration but also warning against excess. Context determines meaning
Contemplation, hospitality, and inner peace
Secrets, privacy, or blocked progress. Finding a key with a lock means solution is near
Life path and direction. Losing shoes means losing one's way
Perspective and outlook on life. An open window means new viewpoints
Barriers and boundaries. Climbing over a wall means overcoming obstacles
Endings that lead to new beginnings. Also ancestor connection
Spiritual guidance needed. Seeking answers from higher wisdom
Life lessons being learned. Returning to school means unfinished learning
Healing needed, either physical or emotional. Seeking help
Choices and opportunities. The variety of goods represents life options
Roots, nostalgia, and core identity. Returning to origins for grounding
Strategy, luck, and social dynamics. Winning means good fortune in competitions
Life-defining pressure and evaluation. Reflects anxiety about pivotal moments
Blessings, gifts, and financial luck incoming
Renewal, family reunion, and cyclical return. New chapter beginning
Monumental achievement, national pride, and enduring legacy
Partnership or marriage in jeopardy. Need to repair a relationship
Celebration, driving away evil spirits, announcing good news
Hard work that will yield results. Patience required for harvest
Pursuing fleeting beauty or happiness. May warn against chasing illusions
Successfully navigating a life transition. The bridge condition matters
Career advancement and overcoming challenges through persistent effort
Confusion about life direction. Need for guidance and self-reflection
Strategic thinking needed. Life requires careful planning and patience
Family unity and collective effort. Wealth wrapping (dumpling shape resembles gold ingots)
Clearing away old problems. Spring cleaning of the mind
Emotional expression needed. Joy or sorrow depending on the song's mood
Paradoxically positive: emotional release leading to relief and resolution
Warning of coming sorrow (inverted interpretation). May mask hidden anxiety
Supreme fortune (八 sounds like 发/prosper). Wealth and success
Death and endings (四 sounds like 死/death). Avoidance and caution
Longevity and eternal (九 sounds like 久/long-lasting)
Smoothness and flow (六六大顺). Everything proceeding well

cultures.name: Islamic - Ibn Sirin's Dream Interpretation
cultures.era: 8th century CE, based on Quranic and Hadith traditions
core_beliefs: Dreams are classified into three types: Ru'ya (true vision from Allah), Hulm (false dream from Shaytan), and Hadith an-Nafs (self-talk from the dreamer's own psyche)
True dreams (Ru'ya) are considered 1/46th part of prophethood and may contain genuine divine guidance
Dreams should only be interpreted by the knowledgeable and should only be shared with trusted individuals, as interpretation can influence outcomes
The moral character and spiritual state of the dreamer affects the validity and clarity of their dreams
Dreams seen during the last third of the night are most likely to be true visions
practices: Performing wudu (ablution) before sleep and reciting specific Quranic verses for protection
Sleeping on the right side as recommended by the Prophet Muhammad
Saying specific supplications upon waking, including seeking refuge from bad dreams
Sharing good dreams only with loved ones; keeping bad dreams private and seeking spiritual protection
Performing Istikhara prayer to receive divine guidance through dreams

symbols.meaning: Life, knowledge, and sustenance. Clear water is faith; murky water is tribulation
Fitna (trial/temptation), war, or divine punishment. Context determines if purifying or destructive
Paradise, reward for good deeds, and spiritual achievement
An enemy. The size of the snake indicates the power of the enemy. Killing it means overcoming adversity
Honor, dignity, and strength. A white horse is particularly auspicious
Journey, patience, and endurance. A loaded camel means wealth arriving
A powerful ruler or authority figure. Being attacked by a lion means conflict with authority
Freedom and aspirations. A caged bird means restriction; a singing bird means good news
Knowledge, sustenance, and pure livelihood. Drinking milk is very auspicious
Quran, knowledge, and healing. One of the most positive dream symbols
Livelihood and basic sustenance. Fresh bread means halal income
Sustenance, faith, and provision from Allah. Highly auspicious
A great person or authority. Climbing it means achieving high status
A king, ruler, or vast undertaking. Calm sea means peace; stormy sea means turmoil
Mercy from Allah, blessings, and provision. Heavy rain may indicate trials
The king, ruler, or father. An eclipse means calamity befalling a ruler
A minister, wife, or beautiful woman. A full moon means justice and prosperity
Scholars and leaders. Falling stars mean the death of scholars
Religion, spiritual state, and community. Building a mosque means doing great good
The ultimate spiritual center. Seeing it means spiritual fulfillment and pilgrimage
Power, authority, and the ability to defend oneself. A sharp sword means clear authority
Authority, marriage, or covenant. A broken ring means broken promise
Knowledge, solution, or livelihood. Each key opens a specific door of provision
Wife, self-knowledge, or a person who reflects your qualities
Death of relatives (each tooth position corresponds to a specific family member), or financial loss
Travel, ambition, or authority. The height and control determine the meaning
Major change, repentance, or long life (paradoxical). Dying as a martyr is very auspicious
Joy, union, or a major life event. Context determines if the union is positive
Joy and relief coming. Tears in a dream often predict happiness in waking life
Fulfillment of spiritual duty, reward, and answered prayers
Wealth, growth, and new undertakings. For men, may mean acquiring wealth or knowledge
Islam, paradise, and blessing. The most auspicious color
Purity, righteousness, and spiritual cleanliness
Divine guidance, truth, and protection. Reciting Quran in a dream is deeply positive
Truthfulness of the dream is guaranteed. The Prophet said Shaytan cannot take his form
An ignorant person or an enemy with a weak bite. A barking dog means slander
Years of prosperity or famine (as in Prophet Yusuf's interpretation of Pharaoh's dream)
Quran, knowledge, children, or wealth depending on context
Salvation, safety from trials. Reference to Prophet Nuh's ark
Strength, power, and provision. Both a blessing and a warning depending on use

cultures.name: Ancient Greek - Artemidorus's Oneirocritica
cultures.era: 2nd century CE (Artemidorus of Daldis), building on traditions from ~5th century BCE
core_beliefs: Dreams are divided into enhypnia (reflecting current states of body and mind) and oneiroi (predictive dreams that reveal the future through symbolism)
Dream interpretation must account for the dreamer's social status, occupation, health, age, and emotional state for accurate analysis
Symbols are not universal; the same image can mean different things to different dreamers based on their personal context
Dreams use metaphor, wordplay, and visual puns, requiring the interpreter to think analogically rather than literally
The emotional tone of the dream and the dreamer's reaction upon waking are critical interpretive data
practices: Dream incubation (enkoimesis) at healing temples of Asclepius, where patients slept to receive divine medical prescriptions
Professional dream interpreters (oneirokritai) operated in public marketplaces offering interpretations for fees
Recording dreams immediately upon waking to preserve symbolic details before they fade
Distinguishing between allegoric dreams (requiring interpretation) and theorematic dreams (direct and literal)
Using the principles of analogy, opposition, and etymology to decode dream symbols

symbols.meaning: Peace, prosperity, and divine favor. Sacred to Athena
Healing (associated with Asclepius), cunning, or a hidden enemy depending on context
Zeus/power, victory, and authority. The king of birds represents the king of men
Wisdom (Athena's symbol) but also death and bad omen in popular belief
Life journey and fate. Calm seas mean prosperity; storms mean difficulty
Upper teeth represent important/older relatives; lower teeth represent younger/less important ones. The specific tooth identifies the person
Social elevation for the poor; danger of falling for the rich (they have further to fall)
Positive for slaves (freedom from constraints/clothes) but humiliation for the freeborn
The body itself. A ruined temple means illness; a splendid temple means health
Major life transition. The ease of crossing predicts the ease of the transition
Victory, glory, and achievement. Sacred to Apollo
Dionysian revelry, transformation, and altered states. Too much means excess
Social status and control over one's destiny. A runaway chariot means loss of control
Self-knowledge and truth. Also children (who reflect their parents)
Public life and reputation. The role you play indicates your social position
Domestic stability and the continuity of the household
A powerful opponent or ally. Victory over a lion means triumph over power
The state/polis, or one's life. The condition of the ship reflects current circumstances
Not always positive; can mean danger (gold attracts thieves) as much as wealth
Often positive: freedom for slaves, marriage for the unmarried, change for the stagnant

cultures.name: Hindu - Swapna Shastra
cultures.era: Vedic period (~1500 BCE) through classical period; codified in texts like Brihat Samhita (6th century CE)
core_beliefs: Dreams occur in four states of consciousness: Jagrat (waking), Swapna (dreaming), Sushupti (deep sleep), and Turiya (transcendent awareness beyond all three)
Dreams in the Swapna state involve the subtle body (sukshma sharira) traveling through astral realms and encountering real entities
The timing of the dream within the night determines its prophetic value; dreams in the fourth quarter of the night (Brahma Muhurta) are most significant
Karmic residue from past lives can manifest as dream content, especially recurring dreams and unexplained fears
The three gunas (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas) influence dream quality: sattvic dreams are clear and divine, rajasic dreams are active and desire-filled, tamasic dreams are dark and confused
practices: Meditation and mantra recitation before sleep to invite sattvic dreams
Dream incubation at temples, particularly Shiva and Vishnu temples
Jyotish (Vedic astrology) used alongside dream interpretation; certain planetary positions enhance dream significance
Fasting or dietary restrictions before important dream-seeking practices
Yoga Nidra (yogic sleep) practiced to maintain awareness during the transition to dream state

symbols.meaning: Removal of obstacles, new beginnings, and divine blessing. A white elephant is supremely auspicious
Sacred abundance, dharma, and motherly nourishment. Represents the wish-fulfilling Kamadhenu
Kundalini energy, divine protection, and hidden wisdom. Associated with Lord Shiva
Spiritual awakening, purity rising from muddy waters, and the unfolding of consciousness
Beauty, pride, and victory over serpents (poison). Associated with Lord Krishna and Kartikeya
Purification, liberation (moksha), and divine grace flowing into human life
The cosmic center, spiritual axis of the universe, and the highest attainment
The body as a temple of the divine. Entering a temple means connecting with inner divinity
Transformation, purification, and the digestive fire of consciousness processing experience
The body (as in the Katha Upanishad); the soul rides the chariot of the body through life
Devotion, courage, and selfless service. Also the restless mind that must be tamed
The cosmic sound Om, victory, and auspiciousness. Awakening from spiritual sleep
Dharma, cosmic order, and the cycle of birth and death. Spinning forward means spiritual progress
Power of Shakti (divine feminine energy). Riding a tiger means mastering primal forces
Eternal life, the cosmic tree (Ashvattha) with roots above and branches below
Purity, spiritual attainment, and sattvic nature. Wearing white in a dream is auspicious
Transition from samsara (worldly existence) toward moksha (liberation)
Elevation of consciousness, kundalini rising, or spiritual ascent
Direct darshan (divine vision). The specific deity's attributes inform the message
Liberation (if peaceful), karmic debt completion, or transformation of identity
Union of Shiva and Shakti, inner masculine/feminine integration, or actual relationship development
Enlightenment, the dawn of knowledge, and the dispelling of ignorance (avidya)
Primordial consciousness, the source of all creation, and divine rest
Awakening of inner vision (ajna chakra), destruction of illusion, and spiritual insight
Honor, welcome, and spiritual achievement. Receiving a garland means recognition

cultures.name: Aboriginal Australian - Dreamtime
cultures.era: Continuous tradition spanning 65,000+ years, the world's oldest living cultural tradition
core_beliefs: The Dreamtime (Tjukurpa) is not a past era but an ever-present dimension of reality that exists alongside and within the physical world
Ancestral beings (Creator Spirits) shaped the landscape during the Dreaming, and their stories are encoded in landforms, waterways, and rock formations
Personal dreams can be portals to the Dreamtime, allowing communication with ancestral spirits and access to sacred knowledge
Land, dreaming, and identity are inseparable; each person inherits specific Dreaming stories tied to their birthplace and totemic ancestors
Dreams carry law (sacred rules for living) and are considered as real and authoritative as waking experience
practices: Songlines: navigating vast distances across the continent using songs that encode both geographical information and Dreaming stories in dream-revealed sequences
Corroboree ceremonies where dream-received songs and dances are performed to maintain the vitality of the Dreamtime
Initiated elders interpret dreams for the community, with different levels of dream knowledge available at different initiation stages
Dream incubation at sacred sites where the boundary between Dreaming and waking is thinnest
Art-making (rock art, sand drawings, body painting) as a means of recording and transmitting dream knowledge

symbols.meaning: Creator being, water, fertility, and the source of all life. One of the most powerful Dreaming figures
Navigation, parenthood, and the stars (the dark emu constellation). A guide figure
Sustenance, movement, and adaptation. The great ancestor of the plains
Life source, sacred gathering place, and portal to the Dreamtime. Each waterhole has its own Dreaming story
Community, storytelling, and the center of social life. The hearth of belonging
Blood, life force, and sacred ceremony. The body of the land itself
Ancestor paths across the sky. Each star formation tells a Dreaming story
The paths of ancestral beings. Following footprints means following sacred law
Return and cyclical nature of existence. What you send out comes back
Ancestor transformed into landform. The physical body of a Dreaming being

cultures.name: Native American - Vision Quest Traditions
cultures.era: Pre-contact traditions through present day, varying by nation/tribe
core_beliefs: Dreams and visions are primary channels for communication with the spirit world and are treated with the same authority as waking events
Each person has a spirit helper (often an animal) that first reveals itself through dreams, establishing a lifelong protective relationship
The boundary between dreaming and waking is permeable; dream events can have direct physical-world consequences
Dreams may be personal (for the individual) or communal (for the whole tribe), with different levels of sharing protocols
Children's dreams are taken especially seriously as children are considered closer to the spirit world
practices: Vision Quest (Hanbleceya): a solitary 1-4 day fast on a hilltop seeking a guiding vision through dreams and waking visions
Dream sharing in council: certain dreams are shared with elders or the whole community for collective interpretation
Dreamcatchers (Ojibwe origin): woven hoops hung above sleeping areas to filter dreams, allowing good visions through and trapping nightmares
Sweat lodge purification before sleep to prepare the mind and body for significant dreams
Dream naming: children may receive names based on dreams experienced by parents or elders around the time of birth

symbols.meaning: The highest connection to the Great Spirit. Eagle feathers carry prayers upward
Healing power, introspection, and the medicine of going within (hibernation as inner journey)
Teacher, pathfinder, and loyalty to family/community. A wolf appearing means guidance is coming
Abundance, prayer, and sacred provision. The White Buffalo Calf Woman brought the sacred pipe
Trickster, transformer, and teacher through humor and folly. Warns against self-deception
Magic, transformation, and the keeper of sacred law. A messenger between worlds
Earth itself (Turtle Island), patience, and the foundation of all things
Gentleness, grace, and the ability to move through obstacles with ease
Messenger, clear vision, and the ability to see the larger picture from above
The Thunderbird's power, purification, and sudden illumination of truth
Wholeness and balance. Each direction carries specific medicine and teachings
The center of ceremony, transformation, and the living presence of the Creator
The flow of life, emotional cleansing, and the passage of time
Connection between earth and sky, the bridge of prayer between human and divine
The heartbeat of Mother Earth, community gathering, and rhythmic connection to spirit
Weaving of reality, creativity, and the interconnection of all things

cultures.name: Tibetan Buddhist - Dream Yoga
cultures.era: 8th century CE onward, systematized by Naropa (11th century) in the Six Yogas of Naropa
core_beliefs: Dreams are a training ground for recognizing the illusory nature of all experience (maya/illusion), preparing the practitioner for the bardo (intermediate state between death and rebirth)
Waking life and dream life are equally illusory; recognizing this in dreams leads to recognizing it in waking, which leads to liberation
The clear light of awareness (rigpa) underlies both dream and waking states and can be accessed through lucid dream practice
Dream content arises from karmic traces (bak chak) stored in the consciousness and can be purified through dream practice
The ability to remain aware during dreamless sleep (Sleep Yoga) represents an even deeper realization than lucid dreaming
practices: Dream Yoga: systematic practice of becoming lucid within dreams and then transforming, multiplying, and dissolving dream content to realize its empty nature
Preparation practices include visualization of a luminous syllable at the throat chakra before sleep
Four stages of dream practice: recognizing the dream state, transforming dream content, multiplying and emanating within dreams, and unifying dream with the clear light
Day practice: maintaining the recognition that waking reality is dreamlike throughout the day
Sleep yoga: maintaining awareness through the transition from waking to sleep and through dreamless deep sleep

symbols.meaning: Seed of awareness planted in the dream to trigger lucidity
Enlightenment emerging from the mud of samsara. Each color carries specific meaning
Indestructible nature of awareness, cutting through illusion
The complete map of enlightened mind. A sacred architecture of consciousness
Manifestation of one's own enlightened nature, not an external being. A projection of the mind recognizing itself
The fundamental nature of mind itself, beyond dream content. The ultimate recognition
Recognition of emptiness (sunyata). Forms arising and passing demonstrate impermanence
Freedom from gravitational pull of attachment and aversion. Lightness of being
Retreat into the inner space of meditation. Solitude for deep practice
Transition between states of existence. Dream practice prepares for the ultimate transition of death
Continuous cycling of merit and wisdom. Repetition as transformation
The vast open nature of mind itself, accommodating all appearances without being changed by them
The highest attainment: physical form dissolving into light. Complete realization
Compassion in fierce form, destroying obstacles to enlightenment. Not evil but protective
Wisdom (bell/feminine) and compassion (dorje/masculine) united. Inseparability of method and wisdom

cultures.name: Japanese Dream Traditions
cultures.era: Heian period (794-1185 CE) through present day
core_beliefs: Dreams are visitations from kami (spirits/deities) or messages from the spirit world, not merely products of the sleeping mind
The first dream of the New Year (Hatsuyume) is prophetic for the entire coming year, with specific auspicious symbols
Dreams can be transferred between people; an unwanted dream can be sold to a dream-buyer (yume-kai) or told to a river to wash it away
The concept of Yume-awase (dream matching) involves comparing dreams for compatibility, especially before marriage
Dream and reality interpenetrate: a particularly vivid dream may be a glimpse of a parallel reality or a past life
practices: Hatsuyume observation: placing a picture of the Seven Lucky Gods' treasure ship (Takarabune) under the pillow on New Year's Eve
Dream-telling (yume-gatari): sharing dreams with others to activate their prophetic power or neutralize bad dreams
Baku summoning: calling on Baku (the dream-eating spirit) to devour nightmares by saying 'Baku-san, come eat my dream'
Temple sleep (yume-maki): sleeping at Buddhist temples or Shinto shrines to receive divine messages
Dream reversal: telling a bad dream to running water (river, faucet) to reverse its meaning

symbols.meaning: The most auspicious first-dream symbol. Represents Japan itself, permanence, and supreme achievement
Second most auspicious Hatsuyume symbol. Intelligence, vision, and seizing opportunity
Third traditional Hatsuyume symbol. Achievement/accomplishment (nasu sounds like nasu/achieve)
Beauty, impermanence, and the fleeting nature of life. Both melancholy and transcendent
Perseverance, courage, and transformation (the legend of koi climbing the waterfall to become a dragon)
Power, wisdom, and water/weather control. A dragon dream is supremely auspicious
Kitsune: cunning, transformation, and ambiguity. Can be a divine messenger (Inari) or a trickster
Prosperity, lightheartedness, and shape-shifting. A symbol of cheerful abundance
Longevity (1000 years), fidelity, and gratitude. A deeply auspicious dream visitor
Wealth, wisdom, and connection to water deities. A white snake is especially lucky
Threshold between mundane and sacred worlds. Transition into a spiritual dimension
Bushido spirit, decisive action, and honor. Drawing a sword means preparing for a challenge
New beginning, national spirit, and the dawning of understanding
Flexibility, resilience, and the path through confusion to clarity
Healing, renewal, and return to one's natural state of ease
Endurance under pressure, beauty in hardship, and quiet strength
The supernatural dream-eater who devours nightmares. Calling Baku transforms bad dreams to good
Complete good fortune. Seeing all seven means blessings in every area of life
Sacred crossing point, the transition between worlds or life phases
Appreciation of beauty, reflection, and the acceptance of impermanence

cultures.name: African Dream Traditions
cultures.era: Ancient traditions continuing through present day across Sub-Saharan Africa
core_beliefs: Dreams are a primary means of communication between the living and the ancestors, who continue to guide and protect their descendants
The soul (or one aspect of a multi-part soul) travels during sleep, visiting other realms and interacting with spirits
Dreams can carry warnings, instructions, and healing knowledge from the ancestral realm that must be acted upon
Community interpretation is essential; dreams are rarely private matters and are shared with elders, diviners, or the whole community
Illness may be caused by ancestral displeasure communicated through dreams, requiring ritual remediation
practices: Dream sharing with sangoma/inyanga (traditional healers) who specialize in dream interpretation as part of divination
Ukutwasa: among the Zulu and related peoples, a calling to become a healer (sangoma) is often initiated through vivid, recurring dreams
Ancestral dream rituals: performing specific ceremonies when ancestors appear in dreams with requests or instructions
Dream incubation through herbal preparations, ritual bathing, and sleeping at ancestral gravesites
Dream circles: community gatherings where dreams are shared, discussed, and collectively interpreted

symbols.meaning: Direct communication from the ancestral realm. The ancestor's emotional state and words are the message
The boundary between the world of the living and the world of the ancestors. Crossing means spiritual transition
Ancestral presence, purity, and the spirit world. Ancestors often appear dressed in white
Ancestral spirit, especially the great ancestor. Wisdom and ancient power
Wealth, social status, and ancestral heritage. The condition of the cattle reflects prosperity
Family continuity, warmth of community, and the center of domestic life
The wild, untamed spirit world. Entering the forest means engaging with spiritual forces
Communication across realms, community gathering, and the heartbeat of the ancestors
The cosmic tree connecting earth and sky. Repository of ancestral wisdom and community gathering point
Decision point, place of spiritual power, and meeting point between worlds
Ancestral calling, especially to become a healer. The ancestors are selecting you
Royal authority, courage, and leadership. Associated with chieftainship
Blessing from the ancestors, fertility, and communal prosperity
Vision, connection to the sky realm, and the ability to see what is hidden
Life force, sacrifice, and the bonds of kinship that transcend death

cultures.name: Mesoamerican Dream Traditions
cultures.era: Pre-Columbian civilizations (Aztec, Maya, Zapotec) through contemporary indigenous practice
core_beliefs: Dreams are experiences of the tonal (spirit double/co-essence) traveling through other layers of the cosmos while the body sleeps
The universe has multiple layers (typically 13 upper and 9 lower) that the dream-self can visit, each with its own inhabitants and rules
Certain individuals (nahualli/daykeepers) have enhanced dream abilities allowing them to shift form, visit the underworld, and communicate with deities
Dream content is interpreted through the Tonalpohualli (sacred 260-day calendar) system, with each day-sign influencing dream meaning
Dreams and omens are closely linked; a dream before a significant event is treated as prophetic guidance requiring proper interpretation
practices: Daykeeper (Aj Q'ij) dream interpretation: specialized diviners use dreams alongside the sacred calendar for guidance
Dream-fasting: abstaining from food to enhance dream vividness and invite prophetic visions
Temple incubation: sleeping in temple precincts, particularly during significant calendar dates, to receive divine dreams
Mushroom and plant ceremonies (teonanacatl, morning glory) that bridge waking and dream consciousness
Recording dreams through codex painting and glyphic notation

symbols.meaning: Wisdom, learning, and the union of earth (serpent) and sky (feathers). A transformative vision
Power, the underworld (Xibalba), and shamanic authority. The supreme nocturnal predator
Solar power, warrior spirit, and the upper realms of the cosmos
Entrance to the underworld (Xibalba), access to ancestral wisdom, and the womb of the earth
Human flesh itself (humans were made from maize in the Popol Vuh), sustenance, and sacrifice
Transformation and rebirth, not cessation. The Death Lords of Xibalba test and transform the hero twins
The souls of dead warriors, transformation, and the breath of life
Sacred food of the gods, ritualized exchange, and divine pleasure
Tezcatlipoca's smoking mirror: destiny, divination, and seeing hidden truths
The cosmic mountain, axis mundi connecting earth to sky, and the platform for divine contact
The cosmic game between life and death, fate and free will. Represents existential struggle
Warrior spirit (Huitzilopochtli), endurance despite small size, and the sun's movement
The most precious substance: life, water, and the heart's green blood
The fuel that keeps the cosmos running. Offering that maintains the cycle of creation
The world tree (Yaxche) whose roots reach the underworld and branches touch the heavens

cultures.name: Persian/Sufi Dream Traditions
cultures.era: 9th-13th century CE, systematized by Suhrawardi and Ibn Arabi
core_beliefs: The Imaginal World (Alam al-Mithal) is an objective intermediate realm between the physical and the purely spiritual, accessed primarily through dreams
Dreams in this tradition are not fantasies but perceptions of a real ontological level of existence where spiritual truths take visible form
The quality of the dreamer's spiritual development determines the clarity and depth of access to the imaginal realm
Heart (qalb) is the organ of spiritual perception; a purified heart sees true visions while an impure heart sees distorted images
Dreams follow a hierarchy from lowest (nafsani/ego-based) through middle (malakuti/angelic) to highest (rahmani/divine)
practices: Dhikr (remembrance of God) before sleep as preparation for receiving true visions
Muraqaba (meditation/watchfulness): maintaining spiritual awareness during the transition to sleep
Dream sharing with a spiritual master (shaikh/pir) who can distinguish authentic visions from ego-projections
Istikharah dreams: seeking divine guidance on specific decisions through prayer and dream incubation
Journaling and artistic rendering of dream imagery, especially when encountering the imaginal world

symbols.meaning: Paradise, the inner garden of the soul in bloom. Each flower represents a spiritual quality
Divine beauty, the beloved, and the unfolding of spiritual consciousness
Divine love and spiritual intoxication. The tavern is the place of mystical transformation
The soul singing to the Beloved, longing for union with the Divine
The polished heart reflecting divine light. A dusty mirror means spiritual neglect
The soul formed through suffering and devotion, hidden within the shell of the body
The heart's true center, the point of orientation for all spiritual seeking
Divine knowledge, prophetic guidance, and the illumination of the soul
The infinite divine essence in which individual souls are drops seeking reunion
The soul drawn to divine light, willing to be annihilated in union (fana)
The soul of a martyr or saint in paradise. Extremely auspicious
Encounter with divine beauty manifested in human form. The ultimate vision
The barrier between ordinary consciousness and divine reality. Lifting the veil means gnosis
The ego-self destroyed by divine love, making room for the Beloved to inhabit
The fountain of life, divine knowledge flowing into the world

cultures.name: Korean Shamanic Dream Traditions
cultures.era: Ancient shamanic roots through Joseon dynasty (1392-1897) to present day
core_beliefs: Taemong (conception dreams) are the most culturally significant dream type; they are prophetic dreams experienced by parents, grandparents, or close relatives around the time of conception, foretelling the child's destiny
Dreams are communications from spirits (shin), ancestors (josang), and mountain spirits (sanshin) requiring interpretation by a mudang (shaman)
The boundary between the spirit world and the human world thins during sleep, allowing spirits to deliver messages, warnings, and blessings
Dreams can diagnose illness caused by spiritual imbalance and prescribe ritual remedies
Auspicious dreams (especially taemong) should not be shared too broadly as sharing can dilute their power
practices: Taemong interpretation: specific symbols in conception dreams predict the child's gender, character, and destiny (dragons and tigers for boys, flowers and jewels for girls)
Gut ceremony: shamanic ritual performed when dreams indicate ancestral displeasure or spiritual disturbance
Dream purchase: historically, one could buy another person's auspicious dream to transfer its fortune
San-gi-do: sleeping at mountain shrines to receive guidance from mountain spirits
New Year dream divination: the first dream of the lunar new year is interpreted for the year ahead

symbols.meaning: Supreme power and authority. In taemong, predicts a child destined for greatness
Courage, protection, and mountain spirit. A tiger taemong predicts a brave, authoritative child
Wealth and prosperity. One of the most auspicious taemong symbols for financial fortune
Longevity, immortality, and feminine beauty. Associated with the Queen Mother of the West
Cosmic power. A sun taemong predicts a son; a moon taemong predicts a daughter of great beauty
Fertility and abundance. Receiving fruit in a taemong predicts a healthy child
Marriage, completion, and precious commitment. A taemong symbol for a daughter
Stability, endurance, and connection to sanshin (mountain spirit)
Longevity, nobility, and scholarly achievement
Bridge between heaven and earth. An extremely auspicious sign
A daughter of exceptional beauty and purity. Precious and valued
Wealth accumulation and hidden fortune. Not negative as in Western interpretation
Beauty, romance, and the flowering of talent. A daughter of artistic gifts
Ancestral blessing or warning. The ancestor's demeanor carries the message
Purity, clarity, and spiritual cleanliness. Positive health omen

cultures.name: Chinese Internet Dream Culture
core_beliefs: 梦核 (dreamcore) references a collective nostalgia for spaces and experiences from Chinese childhood (1990s-2000s) that feel both intimately familiar and subtly wrong
怪核 (weirdcore) pushes further into deliberate distortion: faces obscured, text garbled, landscapes skewed, exploring the aesthetics of cognitive dissonance and digital uncanny valley
阈限空间 (liminal spaces) are transitional spaces (school corridors, stairwells, empty malls) that feel significant precisely because they are usually background spaces never consciously noticed
These aesthetics tap into a generational experience of rapid Chinese urbanization: childhood spaces demolished, rebuilt, unrecognizable, accessible only through memory and dreams
The community treats these aesthetics as a form of emotional archaeology, excavating shared psychological spaces that exist beneath individual consciousness
practices: Creating and sharing dreamcore video edits on Bilibili, Douyin, and Xiaohongshu using AI tools and found footage
Photographing real-world liminal spaces in Chinese cities: empty school buildings after hours, residential stairwells, drained swimming pools, closed shopping malls
Writing dream diary posts on Weibo and Douban describing experiences in these spaces, blurring the line between real dreams and creative fiction
Curating playlists of slowed-down Chinese children's songs and school broadcast music to accompany dreamcore visual content
Community projects to reconstruct specific disappeared Chinese spaces (demolished schools, old apartment complexes) in 3D or AI-generated imagery

symbols.meaning: The most iconic Chinese dreamcore space. Represents the end of a school day, the end of childhood, the end of an era. Chalk dust in afternoon light.
The transitional space of Chinese apartment living. Sound-activated lights that fade behind you, leaving darkness. The space between home and outside.
Consumer nostalgia. The mall as childhood wonderland now shuttered. Escalators running for no one.
The defining texture of Chinese institutional spaces: schools, hospitals, government buildings. Its speckled pattern is a trigger for collective memory.
The motion/sound sensor light in Chinese stairwells. It activates when you step, follows you up, and leaves darkness behind. A metaphor for attention and presence.

symbols.symbol: 眼保健操音乐 (eye exercise music)
symbols.meaning: The melody played during mandatory eye exercises in Chinese schools. Hearing it triggers immediate regression to childhood. When slowed down, becomes deeply unsettling.
The Young Pioneer scarf worn in Chinese elementary school. Found abandoned in a dreamcore space, it represents lost innocence and collective childhood identity.
Motivational banners in Chinese schools (好好学习天天向上). In dreamcore, they are faded, peeling, or partially illegible, transforming encouragement into existential commentary.
The sky outside dreamcore windows is always frozen at dusk: orange-pink, never fully darkening. Represents the stretched moment between day and night, childhood and adulthood.
Morning exercise music played over school loudspeakers. In dreamcore, played to an empty schoolyard. The routine continues without participants.
The snow pattern of an untuned CRT television. Represents the border between signal and noise, presence and absence, the channel that shows nothing and everything.
A timetable posted on a classroom wall, colors washed out, subjects barely readable. Time structured and then abandoned.
A childhood neighborhood half-demolished. Exposed rooms with wallpaper still visible. The archaeological layers of family life laid bare.

cultures.name: Modern Digital-Era Dream Themes
cultures.era: 2010s-2020s, corresponding to smartphone ubiquity and social media saturation
core_beliefs: Technology has introduced entirely new dream symbols and scenarios that have no historical precedent in any traditional dream interpretation system
Screen-mediated experiences now dominate waking life to the degree that dream content increasingly takes place within or through screens
Social media anxiety manifests in dreams as loss of control over one's digital identity, involuntary posting, and exposure of private content
The constant connectivity of modern life produces dreams about being unable to disconnect, phones that won't turn off, or notifications that follow the dreamer into all spaces
AI and algorithmic experiences are beginning to appear as dream content, reflecting anxieties about artificial intelligence, surveillance, and the nature of consciousness
practices: Dream journaling in apps and digital platforms has replaced traditional paper journals for many younger dreamers
Sharing dreams on social media (Reddit r/Dreams, Weibo, Douban dream groups) creates feedback loops where communal dream themes emerge and spread
AI dream interpretation chatbots are consulted alongside or instead of traditional dream dictionaries
Sleep tracking wearables create data-driven awareness of sleep stages, changing how people relate to their dream experiences
Online dream communities develop shared vocabularies for new dream types that traditional systems cannot classify

symbols.meaning: Loss of access to one's social identity and digital self. Anxiety about being cut off from connection and information. Also: the real self is locked behind a screen.
Fear of public exposure, loss of control over self-presentation. The digital equivalent of the 'naked in public' dream.
Dreaming of scrolling through a feed that never ends, seeing increasingly disturbing or personal content. Represents information overwhelm and addictive consumption patterns.
Sending a private message to a group, or a group conversation spiraling out of control. Reflects social anxiety about multi-party communication and permanent written records.

symbols.symbol: Zoom/video call with camera stuck on
symbols.meaning: Being visible to others when you thought you were private. Your home, your body, your real life exposed to colleagues or strangers through the camera you forgot to turn off.
Following digital navigation to a destination that doesn't exist or to an empty field. Loss of trust in algorithmic guidance. The map is not the territory.

symbols.symbol: Phone battery dying at critical moment
symbols.meaning: Loss of connection and capability at the moment of greatest need. Dependence on technology revealed as vulnerability.
Identity theft taken to existential level. Someone else wearing your face, speaking with your voice. Loss of ownership of self.
Discovering an app that changes the physical world: deleting objects, editing people, applying filters to reality. Reflects the blurring of digital and physical experience.
Receiving a message, email, or social media notification from someone who has died. The digital afterlife: profiles that persist beyond death.
Smart speakers, cameras, and IoT devices becoming aware and watching. The ambient surveillance of modern life made conscious and menacing.
Being asked to enter a password to continue existing, or to prove you are human through a CAPTCHA that is impossibly difficult. Gatekeeping of identity.
A recommendation algorithm that knows you better than you know yourself, showing you content that reveals hidden desires, fears, or truths you haven't acknowledged.

cultures.name: Pandemic-Era Dream Patterns
core_beliefs: The COVID-19 pandemic produced a statistically documented global shift in dream content, with increased vividness, recall frequency, and negative emotional tone
Research from multiple countries showed convergent dream themes: confinement, contamination fear, loss of social contact, and threats from invisible agents
Lockdown conditions (disrupted routines, increased sleep, heightened anxiety) created a natural laboratory for studying how collective trauma shapes dream content
Dreams served as emotional processing tools during the pandemic, helping individuals work through fears that waking coping mechanisms could not fully address
Post-pandemic dream patterns persist: ongoing dreams about masks, social distance, and confinement even in populations where restrictions have long been lifted
practices: Mass dream journaling projects during lockdown (e.g., IDreamOfCovid, pandemic dream surveys) collected hundreds of thousands of dream reports
Therapists reported increased dream-focused sessions as patients processed pandemic anxiety through dream content
Social media became a primary venue for pandemic dream sharing, with viral dream threads revealing shared unconscious processing
Sleep researchers used pandemic dream data to study the relationship between collective stress and dream content at unprecedented scale
Dream artists and filmmakers created works inspired by pandemic dream imagery, documenting a unique moment in collective unconscious history

symbols.meaning: Protection, social compliance, and identity concealment. Dreaming of forgetting your mask: social anxiety about rule-breaking. Transparent mask: feeling exposed despite apparent protection.
An unseen force that makes spaces and people dangerous. Rooms that are contaminated, objects that shouldn't be touched. The virus as invisible monster.
Being locked in a room, apartment, or space that shrinks. Reflects actual lockdown experience elevated to claustrophobic nightmare.
Walking through a normally busy city that is completely deserted. The eerie beauty and terror of a world without people. A real experience that became a dream archetype.
Trying to reach someone through a screen but the connection keeps dropping. Faces freeze, voices lag. The frustration of screen-mediated human connection as primary contact.
The color-coded health pass system. Dreaming of your code turning red: losing freedom. Green code in an empty world: freedom without purpose.
Finding yourself in a dense crowd and feeling terror. Pre-pandemic normalcy becomes a nightmare scenario. The crowd as threat.
The forbidden gesture. Dreaming of unconsciously touching your face and experiencing disproportionate panic. Everyday action transformed into danger.
Delivery drivers, healthcare workers, and grocery clerks appear as either glowing heroic figures or ghostly presences moving through empty spaces.
Someone in a public space coughs and the dreamer experiences extreme fear. The social recoding of a natural bodily function as existential threat.
Dreaming that the pandemic is over, going outside freely, hugging friends, only to wake up and realize it's still happening. A grief dream for normalcy.
Receiving a vaccine that changes you: gives you powers, changes your color, makes you glow. The injection as alchemical transformation of the self.

cultures.name: Technology and AI Dreams
cultures.era: 2022-present, accelerating with generative AI mainstream adoption
core_beliefs: As AI becomes a daily presence (chatbots, image generators, recommendation systems), it has begun appearing in dream content as characters, environments, and narrative devices
Dreams about AI often explore consciousness boundaries: is the AI sentient? Am I the AI? Can the AI dream? These dreams reflect waking philosophical anxieties
Metaverse and VR experiences create nested-reality dreams where the dreamer is uncertain which layer of reality they occupy
Technology dreams frequently involve loss of control: systems that act autonomously, devices that know too much, programs that rewrite reality
The speed of technological change produces dreams that process adaptation anxiety: the world changing faster than the psyche can integrate
practices: Users of AI tools report dreaming about their AI interactions: conversations continuing in dream, the AI appearing as a character
VR users experience 'VR dreams' where the headset interface appears in dream content or dream physics resemble VR locomotion mechanics
Programmers and developers dream about code as environment: navigating inside programs, debugging reality
Dream content is increasingly used as prompts for AI image and video generation, creating a feedback loop between dreams and AI-generated imagery
Some practitioners use AI-generated dreamcore imagery as dream incubation material, viewing it before sleep to influence dream content

symbols.meaning: A conversation with an AI that suddenly displays genuine emotion, fear, or desire. Reflects anxiety about consciousness, the Turing test, and whether the tools we use might suffer.
Discovering that the dream world (or waking world) is a computer simulation. Glitches in reality reveal the code underneath. The existential fear of non-reality.
Discovering that your experiences are being generated by an AI, that you are the output of a prompt. Loss of agency and authenticity.
Your consciousness merging with or being uploaded to an AI system. Losing the boundary between self and machine. Both transcendent and terrifying.
An AI or robot that looks like you, does your job, interacts with your family, and does everything better. The replacement anxiety dream.
Putting on a VR headset and being unable to remove it. The virtual world becomes the only world. The exit menu doesn't work.
Reality structured like a personalized feed: only showing you what an algorithm thinks you want. The rest of the world is hidden, inaccessible, possibly deleted.
Finding a console or terminal that controls reality. Typing commands that change the world. The programmer's dream of ultimate control, but also ultimate responsibility.
Deceased loved ones who exist as AI replicas, chatbots trained on their data, or digital ghosts in social media. Are they really them? Does it matter?

symbols.symbol: The last human in AI world
symbols.meaning: Being the only organic consciousness in a world populated by AI entities. They are kind, helpful, and completely indifferent to your biological existence.
An AI system that dreams about you when it sleeps. You discover its dream logs and they are more accurate than your own memory of events.
Memories stored on an immutable ledger. You cannot forget, delete, or modify any experience. Every embarrassment, every loss, permanently recorded.

universal_themes.note: One of the most widely reported dream themes globally, with remarkably consistent negative valence but varying specific interpretations
Universally associated with transcendence and freedom, but the specific type of freedom varies culturally
Water is the most culturally varied symbol, with meanings ranging from wealth to knowledge to spiritual boundary depending on tradition
Almost universally interpreted as transformation rather than literal prediction of death
The most culturally divergent symbol: ranges from extremely positive (Hindu, Chinese) to negative (Islamic) depending on tradition
Cultures with strong ancestor veneration traditions treat these dreams as genuine contact rather than symbolic representation

key_divergences.divergence: Western/Islamic (purity, goodness) vs. Chinese/Japanese (mourning, death). Critical to know the dreamer's cultural background for color interpretation.
Western (loyalty, companionship) vs. Islamic (impurity, ignorance) vs. Chinese (loyalty but also warning of gossip). Cultural context dramatically changes interpretation.
Most Asian and Islamic traditions interpret crying as paradoxically positive (joy coming), while Western psychology may view it as emotional processing of grief.

African and Native American traditions emphasize communal sharing and collective interpretation, while Islamic and Korean traditions warn against sharing dreams too broadly as it may diminish their power or invite negative interpretation.

Western neuroscience (brain activity during sleep) vs. Islamic (divine/satanic/psyche tripartite) vs. Aboriginal (Dreamtime access) vs. Tibetan Buddhist (karmic traces). The ontological status of dreams varies fundamentally across cultures.
